User Story: As an endpoint engineer, I want the site to open directly to current endpoint job listings so I can start searching immediately.
Expected Behavior: Home imports src/data/jobs.json, removes stale jobs, renders command panel and results without requiring a runtime API call.
Notes: Data audit: Static job board loads from active feed data.

User Story: As a visitor, I want a clear brand link back to the job board home so I can recover from filtered URLs.
Expected Behavior: Topbar shows Endpoint Jobs brand with icon and links to /.
Notes: Data audit: Topbar brand links back home.

User Story: As a visitor, I want to know when the feed was refreshed so I can judge listing freshness.
Expected Behavior: Topbar and footer format feed.updatedAt, or show Pending refresh for invalid dates.
Notes: Data audit: Refresh timestamps format valid and invalid feed dates.

User Story: As an endpoint specialist, I want the page copy and count to confirm this board is scoped to endpoint/client-platform work.
Expected Behavior: Hero copy names macOS, Windows, MDM, UEM, endpoint security, packaging, and automation; tracked-role count uses active non-stale jobs.
Notes: Data audit: Hero copy and tracked count describe endpoint scope.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want to search terms like Jamf or Intune so only relevant matching jobs remain.
Expected Behavior: Search filters title, company, location, summary, description, seniority, employment type, tags, tools, and platforms case-insensitively.
Notes: Data audit: Keyword search checks broad job text.

User Story: As a keyboard user, I want / to focus the search input when I am not already typing.
Expected Behavior: Pressing / focuses the search box unless focus is in an input, textarea, select, contenteditable element, or modifier key is held.
Notes: Browser audit: Slash keyboard shortcut focuses search input in the browser

User Story: As a job seeker, I want matching text highlighted in results so I can see why a job matched.
Expected Behavior: Query fragments of length 2+ are highlighted in job title, company, and summary without regex injection.
Notes: Browser audit: Search result highlighting renders visible mark elements

User Story: As a job seeker, I want to search city, state, country, or remote text so I can narrow jobs geographically.
Expected Behavior: Location query filters normalized raw location plus canonical mapped location labels and workplace text; country searches include jobs stored by city while blank queries disable the filter.
Errors Found: Country queries returned no results when provider locations contained only city names.
Notes: Data and browser audits verify Switzerland returns Zürich listings through persisted country-aware map labels.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want separate Remote/Hybrid/On-site filtering so workplace preference is not mixed with text search.
Expected Behavior: Workplace select supports Any, Remote, Hybrid, On-site and requires exact job.workplace match except Any.
Notes: Data audit: Workplace filter requires exact workplace type.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want to show only listings with pay data so I can prioritize transparent roles.
Expected Behavior: Salary toggle keeps only jobs with salary.min or salary.max; active filter chip appears and can clear it.
Notes: Data audit: Salary-only filter keeps only transparent pay listings.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want to filter by role family so I can focus on endpoint engineering, security, automation, or platform roles.
Expected Behavior: Role select filters exact RoleFamily values, with All endpoint roles disabling the filter.
Notes: Data audit: Role family filter matches exact role families.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want to filter by recent postings so I can prioritize fresh jobs.
Expected Behavior: Freshness supports Any active listing, Last 1 day, Last 7 days, Last 14 days, and Last 30 days using postedAt age.
Notes: Data and browser audits verify the one-day option and posted-age filtering.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want to filter by macOS, Windows, iOS, Android, or Linux so I can focus on my platform.
Expected Behavior: Each platform button toggles aria-pressed and jobs match when any selected platform is included.
Notes: Data audit: Platform multi-select matches any selected platform.

User Story: As a desktop user, I want advanced filters visible in the hero so I can refine without hunting.
Expected Behavior: Desktop filter stack shows seniority, sort, tools, and clear button when filters are active.
Notes: Browser audit: Desktop filter stack is visible and mobile stack is hidden on desktop viewport

User Story: As a mobile user, I want advanced filters collapsed behind More filters so the first viewport stays usable.
Expected Behavior: Mobile uses a details/summary More filters section with active-filter count; desktop version is hidden at mobile breakpoints.
Notes: Browser audit: Mobile filter stack is collapsed behind More filters and expands on tap

User Story: As a job seeker, I want to filter by level so seniority expectations match my target.
Expected Behavior: Seniority select supports All, Associate, Mid, Senior, Staff, Lead, Manager and filters exact job.seniority.
Notes: Data audit: Seniority filter matches exact seniority.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want to sort listings by freshness, pay, or company so I can scan in my preferred order.
Expected Behavior: Newest sorts postedAt descending, compensation sorts salary max/min descending, company sorts company name ascending.
Notes: Data audit: Sort options order by newest, compensation, and company.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want to filter for Jamf, Intune, SCCM, Fleet, Kandji, NinjaOne, and related tools so I can target my stack.
Expected Behavior: Each tool button toggles aria-pressed and jobs match when any selected tool is included.
Notes: Data audit: Tool multi-select matches any selected tool.

User Story: As a user, I want to see and remove active filters individually so I understand why results changed.
Expected Behavior: Every non-default filter creates a removable chip; Clear all restores initial filter state.
Notes: Data audit: Active filter chips expose removable labels and clear actions.

User Story: As a user, I want filter state in the URL so I can share or reload a filtered search.
Expected Behavior: Current filters serialize into q, location, platforms, tools, workplace, salary, seniority, family, freshness, and sort query params; legacy locations and remote params are cleaned on update.
Notes: Data audit: Filter state serializes to shareable URL params.

User Story: As a browser user, I want back/forward navigation to restore filter state.
Expected Behavior: Filter store listens to popstate and custom filter-change events, using useSyncExternalStore snapshots.
Notes: Browser audit: Browser back navigation restores URL-derived filter state

User Story: As a job seeker, I want the results count to update with filters so I know search scope.
Expected Behavior: Results panel shows animated total endpoint opportunities and Daily refresh note.
Notes: Data audit: Results panel exposes count and daily refresh note.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want long result sets paginated so the page stays usable.
Expected Behavior: 20 jobs per page, top and bottom pagination, disabled prev/next at bounds, page buttons with ellipses, and page changes scroll results into view respecting reduced motion.
Notes: Browser audits verify next/direct-page navigation, synchronized controls, boundary disabling, ellipses, summaries, and reduced-motion scroll-to-results behavior.

User Story: As a user, I want a clear empty state when filters return no jobs so I can recover quickly.
Expected Behavior: When no jobs match, panel shows No matching roles, guidance, and Reset filters button.
Notes: Data audit: Empty state offers recovery through reset filters.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want each card to show source, posted date, title, company, workplace, summary, location, age, role family, seniority, and employment type.
Expected Behavior: Each job card renders those fields from Job and formats posted date/freshness labels.
Notes: Data audit: Job card renders core listing details.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want visible salary ranges highlighted when available.
Expected Behavior: Jobs with salary render a DollarSign salary pill with accessible Salary label; jobs without salary omit the row.
Notes: Data audit: Salary pill renders accessible salary label.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want useful full descriptions expandable under a card without duplicating the summary.
Expected Behavior: Description details render only when additional description is materially longer than summary; paragraphs split on line breaks.
Notes: Browser audit: Expandable descriptions open.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want match reasons so I can see why a role belongs on this niche board.
Expected Behavior: Cards display up to three match reasons with check icons.
Fix Status: Fixed: display fallback added; normalizer fallback added
Notes: Initial audit found 99 active jobs with empty stored matchReasons. Frontend now derives display reasons for existing feed rows; future refreshes emit broader reasons. Data audit: Match reasons render on job cards.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want quick tags for matched tools and platforms so I can scan stack fit.
Expected Behavior: Cards show up to eight combined platform/tool tags.
Notes: Data audit: Platform and tool tags render on job cards.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want an Apply link for real jobs and clear attribution for provider terms.
Expected Behavior: Cards show attribution label; applyUrl opens in a new tab with noopener noreferrer, otherwise Seed listing disabled state appears.
Notes: Browser audit: Apply links open externally with safe rel.

User Story: As a user, I want to report issues or open the repository from the page.
Expected Behavior: Topbar exposes a safe external feedback action; footer shows Made by Jorgeasaurus, refresh timestamp, Report / request GitHub issue link, and GitHub repo link.
Notes: Browser audit verifies visible topbar feedback plus footer attribution and safe GitHub links.

User Story: As a visitor, I want the dark lime visual style to feel polished without blocking interaction.
Expected Behavior: Parallax background renders an aria-hidden atmosphere plus a Three.js endpoint signal canvas behind content when motion is allowed.
Notes: Browser audit: Parallax background atmosphere and signal canvas render behind the site content

User Story: As a visitor, I want count changes to feel responsive without hurting screen reader output.
Expected Behavior: SlotText animates visible numbers and is aria-hidden while nearby text provides accessible context.
Notes: Browser audit: Animated count numbers render numeric text in hero and results

User Story: As a mobile user, I want search, filters, and results to fit narrow screens without horizontal overflow.
Expected Behavior: Responsive CSS stacks controls, collapses advanced filters, preserves tap targets, and keeps cards within viewport.
Notes: Browser audit: Mobile viewport has no horizontal document overflow after opening filters

User Story: As an assistive technology user, I want toggle filters to expose pressed state.
Expected Behavior: Reusable ToggleButton emits literal aria-pressed true/false and type button.
Notes: Data audit: Toggle buttons emit explicit pressed state.

User Story: As a site visitor, I want fast static listings without runtime API failures.
Expected Behavior: Page imports typed JobsFeed from src/data/jobs.json during build; no client fetch is required.
Notes: Data audit: Static JSON feed builds without runtime fetching.

User Story: As the site owner, I want listings refreshed automatically and manually so the board stays current.
Expected Behavior: Workflow runs daily at 11:17 UTC and on workflow_dispatch, installs dependencies, refreshes jobs, builds, commits changed jobs JSON, rebases, and pushes.
Notes: Data audit: Refresh workflow runs on schedule and manual dispatch.

User Story: As the site owner, I want to choose providers by env var so source coverage can change without code edits.
Expected Behavior: JOB_PROVIDERS/JOB_PROVIDER choose adapters, de-duped and validated against supported providers; provider URL overrides are supported.
Notes: Data audit: Provider list and URL overrides are configurable.

User Story: As the site owner, I want broad public job-board coverage.
Expected Behavior: Remotive, Arbeitnow, Jobicy, Remote OK, The Muse, and Adzuna adapters normalize public-board responses into endpoint candidates.
Notes: Data audit: Public job-board providers are wired.

User Story: As the site owner, I want direct company ATS coverage for stable employer feeds.
Expected Behavior: Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, and Workable adapters scan configured boards/accounts and normalize job details.
Notes: Data audit: Direct ATS board providers are wired.

User Story: As the site owner, I want targeted coverage for employers visible in LinkedIn examples without scraping LinkedIn.
Expected Behavior: Amazon Jobs, Workday, Jibe, and Activate adapters search configured company endpoints and queries.
Notes: Data audit: Targeted company ATS providers are wired.

User Story: As the site owner, I want optional paid/API aggregators to increase coverage when keys are available.
Expected Behavior: Techmap RSS, TheirStack, SerpAPI, RapidAPI Daily Jobs, RapidAPI LinkedIn, and AI Dev Board are wired behind env vars/secrets and skipped on provider errors without killing successful providers.
Notes: Data audit: Optional API providers are key-gated and non-fatal.

User Story: As the site owner, I want hand-reviewed listings included even if broad providers miss them.
Expected Behavior: Curated Jobs normalizes reviewed listings and reserves feed slots; availability checks drop 404/410 pages, unavailable text, or missing identity text while retaining jobs when transient checks are inconclusive.
Notes: Data audit verifies reservation wiring plus available, unavailable-text, and missing-required-text classification.

User Story: As a visitor, I want unrelated generic IT/software jobs excluded.
Expected Behavior: Normalizer requires endpoint/client-platform/tool signals and rejects entry support, frontline support, app-security, and software product false positives without matching title evidence.
Notes: Data audit: Normalizer accepts endpoint roles and rejects generic software roles.

User Story: As a visitor, I want listings tagged by practical endpoint platforms and tools.
Expected Behavior: Normalizer derives tools from aliases, derives platforms from aliases or defaults to Windows/macOS, and creates tags/match reasons.
Notes: Data audit: Normalizer derives tools, platforms, tags, and match reasons.

User Story: As a visitor, I want consistent filters even when providers use different fields.
Expected Behavior: Normalizer infers workplace, role family, seniority, and employment type from normalized job text when provider fields are absent.
Notes: Data audit: Normalizer infers workplace, role family, seniority, and employment type.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want salary ranges surfaced even when only descriptions include pay data.
Expected Behavior: Refresh preserves structured salary and extracts USD annual salary ranges from summary/description text when missing.
Notes: Data audit: Salary ranges are preserved or extracted from descriptions.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want descriptions readable and not duplicated as summaries.
Expected Behavior: HTML is stripped, line breaks preserved, short descriptions omitted, long descriptions capped, and summary prefixes are removed before expansion.
Notes: Data audit: Descriptions are cleaned, capped, and not duplicated as summaries.

User Story: As a visitor, I want duplicate listings collapsed and React keys stable.
Expected Behavior: Jobs dedupe by normalized title/company/location or source URL; generated IDs include source/account/title plus source URL hash.
Notes: Data audit: Dedupe and stable IDs use source URLs and React job IDs.

User Story: As a visitor, I want the feed to avoid expired listings and stay scannable.
Expected Behavior: Refresh removes jobs stale before fetchedAt and limits output by JOB_MAX_RESULTS while reserving curated slots.
Notes: Data audit: Stale filtering and result cap are enforced.

Feature: Metadata, Open Graph, and Twitter cards
User Story: As the site owner, I want shared links and search snippets to describe the niche board.
Expected Behavior: Layout exports canonical metadata, keywords, Open Graph, Twitter summary image, author, creator, publisher, and stable listing anchors.
Notes: Data audit verifies metadata fields, social image, removed specialty metadata and footer navigation, and stable job anchors.

User Story: As the site owner, I want search engines to understand the job collection page.
Expected Behavior: Home emits escaped JSON-LD for WebSite/SearchAction, Organization, CollectionPage with up to 20 current listings, WebApplication, and BreadcrumbList; listing sameAs uses apply URL or source URL.
Notes: Data audit verifies graph types, 20-item cap, total count semantics, listing anchors, sameAs fallback, removed popular links, and escaped script content.

User Story: As a crawler, I need sitemap and robots endpoints that point to the canonical site.
Expected Behavior: Sitemap returns siteUrl with feed.updatedAt, daily frequency, priority, and social image; robots allows all plus Googlebot and references canonical sitemap and host.
Notes: Data audit verifies sitemap dates/images and robots wildcard, Googlebot, sitemap, and host rules.

User Story: As the site owner, I want basic page analytics without adding app code to each page.
Expected Behavior: Root layout includes Vercel Analytics component globally.
Notes: Data audit: Vercel Analytics is installed globally.

User Story: As the maintainer, I want PRs scanned for React quality regressions.
Expected Behavior: React Doctor GitHub Action scans pull requests and main pushes, comments/statuses advisory findings, and cancels superseded runs.
Notes: Data audit: React Doctor CI is configured for PRs and main.

User Story: As a user, I want a guided issue form for missing jobs, stale listings, bugs, and feature requests.
Expected Behavior: Issue template requires request type, summary, details, and optional reproduction, device, screenshots, and job examples.
Notes: Data audit: GitHub issue template captures support and feature reports.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want the heavy job map hidden until I ask for it so listing search stays fast.
Expected Behavior: Job geography shows the mapped count and a Show map button by default; clicking expands MapLibre, mounts the canvas, and toggles to Hide map.
Notes: Browser audit: map is collapsed by default and expands on request

User Story: As the site owner, I want verified Greenhouse and Workday employers included so endpoint roles come from direct career sources.
Expected Behavior: Default and scheduled source lists include SpaceX, GitLab, Coinbase, Canonical, Pinterest, Block, and Roblox Greenhouse boards plus Booz Allen, HP, NVIDIA, Adobe, F5, Allstate, Gartner, Nordic Consulting, SHI, Circle, and Jabil Workday sites; README documents representative sources.
Notes: Data audit verifies every named direct source in defaults and scheduled configuration plus README coverage.

User Story: As a visitor, I do not want confirmed unavailable source postings to appear on the page.
Expected Behavior: Built-in exclusions and JOB_EXCLUDED_SOURCE_URLS remove confirmed-dead source URLs during refresh and from runtime active jobs; the reported Adzuna URL is absent from the feed.
Notes: Data audit: dead Adzuna URL is absent, exact/normalized URL exclusion works, refresh filters it, and runtime active jobs reject it.

User Story: As a visitor, I do not want stale Adzuna aggregator results kept by broad source stale windows.
Expected Behavior: Adzuna jobs older than 7 days by fetchedAt or 30 days by postedAt are not active; JSON-LD and job board receive the filtered active feed.
Notes: Data audit: synthetic old-fetched and old-posted Adzuna jobs are inactive while a fresh synthetic Adzuna job remains active.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want to know how many active jobs have usable map locations before opening the map.
Expected Behavior: Job geography computes map points from active jobs and shows mapped jobs plus a mapped-of-total count pill.
Notes: Data audit: mapped count and count pill are sourced from built job map points.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want each mapped job represented as its own point so jobs in the same market do not collapse into one listing.
Expected Behavior: Map point generation skips jobs without coordinates while preserving one point per mapped job, including jobs that share identical coordinates.
Notes: Data audit: per-job map point generation preserves duplicate-coordinate jobs and skips unmapped jobs.

User Story: As a map user, I want zoom controls and a fit-to-jobs action so I can inspect dense job clusters clearly.
Expected Behavior: Expanded maps show zoom in, zoom out, fit-to-jobs controls, and a zoom percentage readout; zoom actions update the readout and respect configured min/max bounds.
Notes: Browser audit: map zoom controls render and zooming updates the readout.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want map points to reveal job title, company, salary, and apply link so the map is actionable.
Expected Behavior: Point and cluster popups are built from GeoJSON feature properties and render title, company, salary label or fallback, and safe external apply links.
Notes: Data audit: popup feature data and rendered popup markup include title

User Story: As a mobile user, I want selected map jobs shown in an in-bounds sheet instead of an off-screen hover popup.
Expected Behavior: Mobile CSS hides the MapLibre popup, renders a bottom sheet with selected job details, and provides a Close selected job button.
Notes: Data audit: mobile sheet and close affordance are wired and mobile CSS hides desktop popups.

User Story: As a visitor, I want the map to match the page style while still attributing map data sources.
Expected Behavior: Map section uses the page lime/emerald palette, 8px rounded framing, dark raster tiles, and visible OpenStreetMap/CARTO attribution links.
Notes: Data audit: map palette

User Story: As the maintainer, I want every job source adapter to share one fetch contract so refresh orchestration can treat sources consistently.
Expected Behavior: ProviderAdapter defines id, displayName, defaultUrl, optional reserved slots, and fetchJobs context; refresh orchestrates adapters through that contract.
Notes: Data audit: provider adapter contract and refresh orchestration are present.

User Story: As the site owner, I want source searches seeded with endpoint-focused titles and monitored companies so refreshes look for the right jobs by default.
Expected Behavior: Default search config includes endpoint/client-platform role queries, monitored endpoint/security companies, and company-specific endpoint query expansion.
Notes: Data audit: endpoint search query defaults and monitored company expansion are present.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want recognizable job locations mapped to real coordinates while broad multi-location rows stay off the map.
Expected Behavior: Refresh map-location resolver maps known cities, regions, countries, and US remote labels to coordinates and skips ambiguous N locations values.
Notes: Data audit: resolver maps known locations and skips ambiguous multi-location rows.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want to compare shortlisted roles so I can evaluate tradeoffs without switching between cards.
Expected Behavior: Users can select 2-4 roles and compare employer, salary, location, workplace, seniority, tools, freshness, and apply links in a responsive table; one-role prompts can clear selection and the shortlist persists while result filters change.
Notes: Data and browser audits verify requested fields, prompt recovery, filter persistence, four-role cap, keyboard access, and mobile internal scrolling without document overflow.

User Story: As a visitor, I do not want broken specialty links in the footer.
Expected Behavior: Footer, site metadata, and JSON-LD omit the removed popular-search links while retaining project navigation.
Notes: Browser audit verifies footer absence; FEAT-051 and FEAT-052 data audits verify metadata and structured-data removal.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want the coverage dashboard to update with my filters so I can judge the size and quality of the current result set.
Expected Behavior: Dashboard keeps the total tracked-role count stable while current-view, mapped, remote-or-hybrid, and salary counts and meter widths recompute from visible filtered jobs; zero-result percentages are zero.
Notes: Data and browser audits verify percentage math and live metric updates after filtering.

User Story: As the site owner, I want invalid or unexpectedly empty refreshes rejected so a bad provider run cannot corrupt the published feed.
Expected Behavior: Feed validation rejects duplicate IDs, missing required attribution/date/match fields, invalid map coordinates, excluded Adzuna jobs, and stored Adzuna snippets; refresh skips writing an empty feed unless JOB_ALLOW_EMPTY is true.
Notes: Data audit exercises validation failures and empty-write policy through pure feed-safety helpers.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want to set a minimum salary so I can focus on roles whose disclosed range can meet my compensation target.
Expected Behavior: The advanced filter stack offers Any salary and $80k-$200k thresholds; selecting a floor keeps USD listings whose disclosed maximum or single salary reaches it, updates the active chip and result count, and persists as minSalary in the URL.
Notes: Data and browser audits independently verify exact and single-value boundaries, non-USD and missing-salary exclusion, reducer state, active chips, URL persistence, removal, and mobile access.

User Story: As an API consumer, I want active endpoint jobs exposed through a stable JSON API so I can build integrations using the same curated data as the site.
Expected Behavior: GET /api/jobs returns active filtered listings with validated UI-compatible filters, pagination metadata, feed timestamp, CORS, and shared cache headers; GET /api/jobs/:id returns one active listing, while invalid queries and missing jobs return structured non-cacheable errors.
Notes: Pure data and live HTTP audits verify filtering, pagination, active-only enforcement, validation, item lookup, error contracts, cache headers, and CORS preflight.

User Story: As a job seeker, I want to filter for leadership roles so I can focus on endpoint management and technical-lead opportunities.
Expected Behavior: A Leadership toggle includes genuine Manager, Director, Head, VP, Chief, and technical Lead titles; excludes obvious product and tool manager false positives; composes with exact seniority; and persists as leadership=1 in UI and API URLs.
Notes: Focused data tests verify independent title classification and seniority inference, filtering, reducer composition, URL/chip behavior, API validation, and OpenAPI; browser audit verifies visible toggle composition, serialization, and hydration.
